<div class="sphinxpress-doc">
<section id="structured-extraction">
<h1>Structured extraction</h1>
<p>Structured extraction is the read-only EPUB inspection API for downstream tools that
need stable source mapping. It is separate from the plain-text reading APIs.
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">epub2txt()</span></code> and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EPUBParser.extract_chapters()</span></code> keep producing cleaned text for
readers, while <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EPUBParser.extract_structured()</span></code> exposes package metadata, spine source
documents, navigation entries, text blocks, inline runs, segments, and diagnostics.</p>
<section id="plain-text-versus-structured-extraction">
<h2>Plain text versus structured extraction</h2>
<p>Plain-text extraction may normalize whitespace, remove duplicate titles, inject readable
separators, or otherwise format content for humans. Structured extraction does not
perform those destructive reading transformations by default. Block text is visible text
only and does not contain internal placeholder tokens such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">__TAG_001__</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="no-epub-rebuilding">
<h2>No EPUB rebuilding</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">epub2text</span></code> only reports what text exists and where it came from. It does not write EPUB
files, rebuild ZIP packages, apply translations, write OPF/NAV/NCX files, or replace
XHTML text. Downstream projects that need writing should use a separate writer package.</p>
</section>
<section id="data-model-overview">
<h2>Data model overview</h2>
<p>The structured API uses dataclasses:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EpubPackageInfo</span></code> for package metadata, manifest items, and spine order.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">SourceDocument</span></code> for decoded spine XHTML/HTML and raw-byte hashes.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">NavigationEntry</span></code> for flattened, deterministic navigation entries.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextBlock</span></code> for prose blocks with source ranges and visible text.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextRun</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">InlineTagRun</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EntityRun</span></code> for ordered content runs.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextSegment</span></code> for sentence, paragraph, or clause slices.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">Diagnostic</span></code> for loss, fallback, and unresolved-reference reporting.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">XhtmlFragment</span></code> for opt-in sanitized inline XHTML attached to blocks and segments.</p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">MarkdownFragment</span></code> for opt-in Markdown translations of inline XHTML attached to blocks
and segments.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="offset-semantics">
<h2>Offset semantics</h2>
<p>Character offsets point into <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">SourceDocument.text</span></code>. Byte offsets are included when a
char-to-byte map can be built for the detected encoding. For exact blocks,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">outer_char_start</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">outer_char_end</span></code> slices the full source element and
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">inner_char_start</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">inner_char_end</span></code> slices the inner source. Text-bearing runs join
to exactly <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextBlock.text</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="xhtml-fragments">
<h2>XHTML fragments</h2>
<p>Call <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EPUBParser.extract_structured(include_xhtml_fragments=True)</span></code> or
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">extract_epub_structure(...,</span> <span class="pre">include_xhtml_fragments=True)</span></code> to attach <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment</span></code>
to each <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextBlock</span></code>. When <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">include_segments=True</span></code> is also set, each <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextSegment</span></code>
receives a fragment for its visible text range. Fragments are rendered from structured
runs, not by serializing BeautifulSoup. They preserve allowed inline tags such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">em</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">strong</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">span</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">a</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">code</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">br</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">wbr</span></code> while omitting block tags, scripts, and
event handlers. <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">XhtmlFragment.text</span></code> is the represented visible text and should match
the visible text of <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">XhtmlFragment.xhtml</span></code>.</p>
<p>The default policy keeps global safe attributes such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">class</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">id</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">lang</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xml:lang</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">dir</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">title</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">epub:type</span></code>. Links may also keep <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">href</span></code>. Disallowed tags
or attributes produce diagnostics such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment_disallowed_tag</span></code> and
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment_disallowed_attr</span></code>. Unbalanced inline markup, opaque inline preservation,
and visible-text mismatches use stable fragment diagnostic codes. <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">strict_offsets=True</span></code>
fails closed on warning or error diagnostics, including fragment diagnostics.</p>
<p>JSON export omits <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment</span></code> by default, even if fragments were generated. Pass
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">include_xhtml_fragments=True</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">to_dict()</span></code> or <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">to_json()</span></code> to include generated
fragments. This keeps the default payload compatible and compact.</p>
</section>
<section id="markdown-fragments">
<h2>Markdown fragments</h2>
<p>Call <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">EPUBParser.extract_structured(include_markdown_fragments=True)</span></code> or
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">extract_epub_structure(...,</span> <span class="pre">include_markdown_fragments=True)</span></code> to attach
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_fragment</span></code> to each <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextBlock</span></code>. When <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">include_segments=True</span></code> is also set, each
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">TextSegment</span></code> receives a fragment for its visible text range. Markdown fragments are
rendered from structured runs, not from serialized XHTML.</p>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">MarkdownFragment.markdown</span></code> is a readable, lossy representation of the same visible text
range as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment</span></code>. The default flavor is <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">commonmark</span></code> and callers may select
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">gfm</span></code> with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_flavor=&quot;gfm&quot;</span></code> or the CLI <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">--markdown-flavor</span></code> option. The first
renderer preserves emphasis, strong text, safe inline links, code-like spans, and hard
line breaks. Unsupported or unsafe inline tags unwrap or drop with diagnostics such as
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_fragment_no_markdown_equivalent</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_fragment_invalid_href</span></code>, and
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_fragment_disallowed_tag</span></code>.</p>
<p>Raw HTML fallback is disabled by default. Treat Markdown fragments as downstream reading
aids for translation, TTS, or agent workflows, not as a lossless EPUB rewrite format.
Use <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">xhtml_fragment</span></code> when exact inline semantics are required.</p>
<p>JSON export omits <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">markdown_fragment</span></code> by default, even if fragments were generated. Pass
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">include_markdown_fragments=True</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">to_dict()</span></code> or <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">to_json()</span></code> to include generated
fragments.</p>
</section>
<section id="cli-examples">
<h2>CLI examples</h2>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>epub2text<span class="w"> </span>extract-structure<span class="w"> </span>book.epub<span class="w"> </span>--xhtml-fragments<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>structure.json
epub2text<span class="w"> </span>extract-structure<span class="w"> </span>book.epub<span class="w"> </span>--markdown-fragments<span class="w"> </span>--markdown-flavor<span class="w"> </span>gfm<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>structure.json
epub2text<span class="w"> </span>extract-structure<span class="w"> </span>book.epub<span class="w"> </span>--segments<span class="w"> </span>sentence<span class="w"> </span>--xhtml-fragments<span class="w"> </span>--markdown-fragments<span class="w"> </span>-o<span class="w"> </span>structure.json
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="diagnostics-and-strict-mode">
<h2>Diagnostics and strict mode</h2>
<p>Diagnostics use severities <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">info</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">warning</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">error</span></code> with stable codes such as
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">missing_nav</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">unresolved_href</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">unresolved_fragment</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">encoding_fallback</span></code>, and
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">offset_unavailable</span></code>. <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">ExtractionPolicy</span></code> includes <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">strict_offsets</span></code> for callers that want
warning and error diagnostics to fail closed.</p>
</section>
<section id="json-export-example">
<h2>JSON export example</h2>
<div class="highlight-python notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">epub2text</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">EPUBParser</span>

<span class="n">parser</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">EPUBParser</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;book.epub&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">extraction</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">parser</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">extract_structured</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">include_segments</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">data</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">extraction</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_json</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">include_raw</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">False</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">include_runs</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">indent</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The JSON export embeds schema <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">epub2text.structured.v1</span></code> and supports omitting raw source
text, runs, or segments for smaller output.</p>
</section>
<section id="downstream-consumer-notes">
<h2>Downstream consumer notes</h2>
<p>Downstream tools should consume block and segment IDs, preserve diagnostics and schema
version, and use source offsets from structured extraction instead of inferring offsets
from cleaned text. Consumers that require lossless rebuilding should fail closed when
diagnostics report non-lossless extraction.</p>
</section>
</section>
</div>
{% endraw %}
